name: Alligator Stew
recipeIngredient: meat well, trimmed and cut into 1 inch cubes
1 tsp Oregano flakes (rubbed between the palms)
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim and cut the alligator into 1 inch cubes. Place in a large pot and cover with salted water. Simmer for about 1 to 1 1/2 hours, then remove and drain well.
2. Put the drained alligator back into the empty pot and cover with milk. Simmer for another 1 to 1/2 hours, then remove and drain well.
3. In a large pot over high heat, saute the alligator in olive oil until all the liquid is gone and the meat starts to brown lightly. Remove from the heat and pour off any excess oil.
4. Add the onions and saute until just crispy tender. Add all the remaining ingredients except the cornstarch, reduce the heat, and simmer covered until the meat is very tender but not falling apart (about 1 to 2 hours). The liquid should cook down by about 1/3.
5. Mix the corn starch with a little water or chicken broth and thicken the sauce if desired. Serve 6 to 8 people.
